response.justification: James Smith is primarily a web and full-stack developer whose career has been almost entirely built on TypeScript, Node.js, JavaScript, and DLang. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project — far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. He has no identifiable fixed-income, fixed-income derivatives, or capital-markets domain experience, and his only tangential financial exposure (RIDE Capital) was in a Node.js/TypeScript context unrelated to trading systems. SQL appears in his skills list without any evidence of SQL Server or Oracle depth. The profile does not meet any of the mandatory criteria for this role.
strengths: Demonstrated CI/CD and DevOps experience across multiple roles, satisfying one optional desirable skill
Java background present (Altom Consulting, robotic-arm work), covering another optional requirement
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing evidence through workshops, conference talks, and course creation
concerns: C++ experience is effectively absent at a professional level — roughly six months at NI in 2015 against a mandatory 5–10 year requirement
Zero capital-markets, fixed-income, or investment-banking domain expertise; the RIDE Capital role was a Node.js web project, not a trading or financial-systems role
No evidence of Windows platform development or SQL Server/Oracle proficiency, both mandatory for the role
